records.ety: [From F. mappe, in mappemonde map of the world, fr. L. mappa napkin, signal cloth; -- a Punic word. Cf. Apron, Napkin, Nappe.]

defs: A representation of the surface of the earth, or of some portion of it, showing the relative position of the parts represented; -- usually on a flat surface. Also, such a representation of the celestial sphere, or of some part of it.
notes: ☞ There are five principal kinds of projection used in making maps: the orthographic, the stereographic, the globuar, the conical, and the cylindrical, or Mercator's projection. See Projection.

defs: A graphical representation of anything showing the relative arrangement of its parts in a maplike form.
Anything which represents graphically a succession of events, states, or acts; as, an historical map.
quotes: Thus is his cheek the map of days outworn.

defs: A relation between two sets in which an element of one set is associated with each element of the other set. Also called a mapping, transformation, or correspondence.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Mapped (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Mapping (?).]

defs: To create a mapping between two sets; see map{4}, n.. Also, to create any list of corresponding elements of two sets of things; as, to map the symbols of an ancient language into a modern phonetic alphabet.
